summary: A frog tells the Queen she will have a daughter within a year.
The King holds a feast for the princess and invites twelve wise women, leaving out the thirteenth.
The thirteenth wise woman curses Rosamond to prick her finger and die, but the twelfth wise woman softens the curse into a hundred-year sleep.
The King orders all spindles in the kingdom to be burned to protect Rosamond.
At fifteen, Rosamond finds an old woman spinning in a tower, touches the spindle, and falls into an enchanted sleep.
The entire castle falls asleep, and a thick hedge of thorns grows around it.
After a hundred years, the Prince reaches the castle as the thorn hedge turns into flowers.
The Prince kisses Rosamond, she wakes, and the whole court wakes with her.
Rosamond and the Prince marry and live happily together.
